title: Devastating earthquake in Venezuela leaves hundreds dead and thousands missing
summary: At least 235 people died and more than 40,000 are on missing lists, while the international community coordinates humanitarian aid.

title: Two typhoons approach Japan, threatening torrential rain and landslides
summary: Typhoons 7 and 8 are heading toward the archipelago, with warnings of heavy rain, possible landslides, and impacts on the bullet train.

title: Japan's national soccer team advances in the World Cup with outstanding performances
summary: Japan shows a solid performance in the tournament, with key players and exciting matches, generating extensive media coverage.

title: Magnitude 5.6 earthquake in Yamanashi causes disruptions but no serious damage
summary: The quake, with a weak 6 intensity on the Japanese scale, did not cause a tsunami or major damage, although it affected Shinkansen service and mobilized authorities.

title: Proposal to reform Imperial Household Law to allow adopted descendants to inherit the throne
summary: The government is studying an amendment that would grant throne eligibility to adopted male children from collateral branches, aiming to stabilize succession.

title: Former mayor of Hino ordered to pay compensation for illegal expenses
summary: The court ordered the former mayor to compensate the municipality for improper payments to a former deputy mayor, in a case of public fund embezzlement.

title: Japanese media companies ally with digital platforms to expand their presence
summary: Fuji HD and TBS seek to strengthen their streaming content offerings through agreements with SBI Holdings and U-Next, respectively.

title: Prices of staple foods in Japan rise, including rice and snacks
summary: Calbee increases prices on 70 products, while rice reaches 3,590 yen per 5 kilograms, reflecting inflationary pressure.

title: Nikkei 225 index suffers historic drop of over 3,000 points
summary: The Tokyo stock exchange recorded its third-largest daily loss, dragged down by a plunge in Asian markets and global economic fears.

title: Government postpones decision on funding for consumption tax reduction
summary: Discrepancies between parties force a delay until year-end in defining how to compensate for revenue loss from a zero tax rate.

title: Former Goldman Sachs employees sanctioned for insider trading in takeover bids
summary: The securities market commission recommends fines for leaking information on public takeover offers in two separate cases.

title: Three center-left parties agree to merge to form a new political force
summary: The Centre Party, the Constitutional Democratic Party, and Komeito create a committee to negotiate unification, although differences remain.

title: Prime Minister Takichi announces new energy policies and creation of a space force
summary: The government promotes a strategy that advances nuclear and renewable energy, and transforms the Air Force into a space branch.

title: New president of Policy Investment Bank bets on collaboration with megabanks
summary: Maki assumes the position with the aim of providing venture capital to innovative sectors and fostering cooperation with the three major banks.

title: Japan's emperor and empress return from their tour of the Netherlands and Belgium
summary: After an official visit, the monarchs expressed their desire that the bonds of friendship with both nations be strengthened.

title: Private jet overshoots runway while landing at Yao Airport in Osaka
summary: The incident left no injuries and authorities are investigating the cause of the runway overshoot.

title: Construction begins on the Chūō Shinkansen maglev train
summary: The high-speed project between Tokyo and Nagoya receives final approval from the governor of Shizuoka, with construction scheduled to start.

title: Prime Minister Takichi denies ordering defamatory videos against opponents
summary: Takichi says he will answer questions about the scandal, amid criticism of the Liberal Democratic Party.

title: Debate in Japan on criminalizing destruction of the national flag
summary: The ruling party proposes sanctions, but the Japan Federation of Bar Associations opposes it, considering it a violation of freedom of expression.

title: Naomi Osaka to make Wimbledon debut against France's Jacquemot
summary: The Japanese tennis player begins her participation in the London tournament in search of her first Grand Slam title on grass.
